pathAssistantSteps.info:
Goal: Finalize acceptance and hand off the scholar cleanly.

  • Confirm eligibility is checked before saving Accepted.
  • Make sure the case owner is set for scholar communication.
  • Record the acceptance rationale and any follow-up commitments.

Goal: Collect the finalist materials needed before review.

  • Most recent resume, including GPA, leadership activities, and athletic achievements.
  • Two letters of recommendation: one from a coach or college professor, and one from the summer internship supervisor.
  • If applicable, financial aid, scholarships, or grants the applicant is receiving or has received.
  • Short essay describing commitment to the CPL mantra, aspirations for a wealth management career, and any personal story that strengthens the application.

Goal: Capture review context and prepare a decision recommendation.

  • Confirm eligibility before moving toward an acceptance decision.
  • Assign the case owner for applicant communication and next steps.
  • Summarize reviewer observations and open questions in internal notes.

Goal: Start the scholarship application from a confirmed invite.

  • Confirm the applicant account is the correct Person Account.
  • Send the finalist invite and record the sent date/status.
  • Keep the applicant in this stage until they confirm participation.

Goal: Close the application with a complete, reportable rejection reason.

  • Select the high-level rejection filter for reporting.
  • Add concise decision context in Rejection Reason.
  • Confirm applicant communication has an owner before closing the file.

Goal: Preserve a clear waitlist decision and next action.

  • Document why the applicant is waitlisted and what would change the decision.
  • Assign ownership for status updates and applicant communication.
  • Review capacity before moving to Accepted or Rejected.

Goal: Record why the applicant exited the process and preserve useful context.

  • Use the rejection fields to categorize and explain the withdrawal.
  • Capture who confirmed the withdrawal and any timing constraints.
  • Leave internal notes useful for future cycles or re-engagement.
